verses.text: In the cave David pours out his complaint, declaring his trouble, for when his spirit was overwhelmed, 'thou knewest my path.' He hides nothing from God. The God who already knows our way welcomes us to pour out the whole of it.

In the cave David pours out his complaint, declaring his trouble, for when his spirit was overwhelmed, 'thou knewest my path.' He hides nothing from God. The God who already knows our way welcomes us to pour out the whole of it.

In the cave David pours out his complaint, declaring his trouble, for when his spirit was overwhelmed, 'thou knewest my path.' He hides nothing from God. The God who already knows our way welcomes us to pour out the whole of it.

Looking around, no man cared for his soul, so he cried, 'Thou art my refuge and my portion in the land of the living.' When all forsook him, God remained. The friendless believer finds in God both shelter and inheritance.

Looking around, no man cared for his soul, so he cried, 'Thou art my refuge and my portion in the land of the living.' When all forsook him, God remained. The friendless believer finds in God both shelter and inheritance.

He begs God to hear and 'bring my soul out of prison,' that he may praise His name among the righteous. He longs for release to worship. The end of every rescue is that the freed soul may give God thanks.

He begs God to hear and 'bring my soul out of prison,' that he may praise His name among the righteous. He longs for release to worship. The end of every rescue is that the freed soul may give God thanks.
